topic: Two-pole geomagnetic model — verification of ISS-1306 patch + duplication finding
our_argument_summary: WIN-053 (Two-pole geomagnetic model). Verdict: Self-Contradicted. We argue the dome's toroidal architecture is topologically a ring magnet, in which flux conservation (∇·B=0) forces a ~1,600:1 magnitude asymmetry between source and return — yet the dome's fitted B(r) gives near-equal coefficients A=62,376 nT and C=64,852 nT at both ends, a 1,660:1 self-contradiction within the dome's own geometry. Independently the exponential B ∝ e^(−r/λ) form is justified post-hoc rather than derived from the toroidal architecture; A, C, and λ are observed-pole values relabeled (no derivation from disc_radius, firmament_height, or moon_altitude); λ is the same parameter pressed into service for an unrelated phenomenon (firmament height H(r)).

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: monitor.py does not track WIN-053. No script computes B(r) from disc/firmament parameters. The 20% RMS improvement claim is asserted on the dome site, not produced by repository code.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Neither — no automation exists for WIN-053 in the dome's repository.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None. detail_evidence states 'monitor.py does not track WIN-053, and no script computes B(r)' — confirmed via grep against current raw-text/monitor.py.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py contains no domain or branch for WIN-053; no B(r), no field-strength comparison, no observatory data fetch tied to this WIN.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: WMM2025 / IGRF-13 / CHAOS-7 already model the geomagnetic field at all latitudes including both polar regions, with degree-13 spherical harmonics achieving ~50 nT global RMS. The dome's ~10,000–13,000 nT RMS (20% of typical field strength) is two orders of magnitude worse. The dome's contribution is renaming the mechanism (geodynamo → toroidal-aetheric flow) while adopting observed pole magnitudes as fit parameters.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Pole-region field strengths (~55,000 nT) have been measured continuously since the 19th century and tabulated in IGRF since 1968; A=62,376 and C=64,852 are post-hoc fit coefficients to those measurements, not pre-data predictions.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation of A, C, or λ from disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km, or moon_altitude=2,534 km exists on the dome site or in monitor.py. λ=8,619 km is also used as the H(r) scale length, but that is parameter sharing across unrelated phenomena, not derivation.

patch_verification.summary: ISS-1306 patch landed cleanly. Multi-commit history reviewed since c9 (Apr 22): c4bfd802 (Apr 23, TLDR/P3 symmetrize-to-geographic-poles + ISS-1306-equivalent earlier rock→mantle attempt) and the 2026-05-10 M1 patch run pushed by queue_id 327. The 2026-05-10 patch run successfully replaced 'thousands of kilometres of rock' with 'thousands of kilometres of mantle and crust' in detail_evidence. Independently of ISS-1306, c9 H0 (TLDR comparators) and c9 H1 (P3 comparators) appear to have been fixed by c4bfd802 — both now use geographic poles, not NMP/SMP. However c4bfd802 introduced a TLDR duplication artifact that survived 17 days un-flagged (see new hole NH-1).

patches.description: Replace 'thousands of kilometres of rock' with 'thousands of kilometres of mantle and crust' in detail_evidence (geological depth-scale accuracy: crust is only 7–35 km, deeper transit is mantle).
patches.verbatim_text_present: propagates upward through thousands of kilometres of mantle and crust to produce the ~55,000 nT actually measured at the geographic south pole
patches.notes: Patch sits in the multi-layer-conduction defender-rebuttal paragraph. Replacement is geologically accurate — at the dome's claimed sub-terranean route, transit indeed crosses both mantle and crust.

patches.target_issue: c9 H0 (TLDR factual error on southern comparator)
patches.description: TLDR previously asserted 'matches to within ∼3%' (against SMP) while detail used geographic south pole — internally inconsistent. c9 flagged as major.
patches.verbatim_text_present: C=64,852 nT is ∼18% above the geographic south pole (∼55,000 nT per WMM2025/IGRF-13)
patches.notes: TLDR now uses 13%/18% against geographic poles, removing the SMP/geographic mismatch. This was fixed by commit c4bfd802 (Apr 23) but never verified by curmudgeon — addressing it here. ISS-1413 (still in open-issues.json) appears to track this same item; recommend decider close ISS-1413 as resolved.

patches.description: P3 previously used NMP for north and geographic south for south — asymmetric. c9 flagged.
patches.verbatim_text_present: ~55,000 nT at the geographic north pole and ~55,000 nT at the geographic south pole per WMM2025 / IGRF-13. Both coefficients overshoot the geographic pole values by ~13–18%
patches.notes: P3 now symmetric (both poles geographic). Fixed by c4bfd802.

holes_found.description: TLDR DUPLICATION ARTIFACT (botched edit from commit c4bfd802 Apr 23, 17 days un-flagged). tldr_evidence currently reads (verbatim): '...forces one of those poles to be ~1,600× weaker than the other. Both coefficients were fitted to observed WMM pole strengths — Both coefficients were fitted to observed pole-region field strengths — A=62,376 nT is ∼13% above the geographic north pole...' Two near-identical em-dashed clauses ('Both coefficients were fitted to observed WMM pole strengths' / 'Both coefficients were fitted to observed pole-region field strengths') sit back-to-back. Diff inspection of c4bfd802 confirms the editor added the second clause as a replacement intended to remove the SMP-coupled '3% match' framing, but failed to delete the original lead-in. Severity moderate (not major) because the meaning is still discernible — it's editorially embarrassing, not factually wrong. A dome defender quoting the TLDR can ridicule the review for visible un-edited duplication ('did anyone proofread this?'). The c8 and c9 reviewers did not catch this because c9 was written before c4bfd802; nothing has triggered a TLDR re-read since.
holes_found.recommendation: Patch tldr_evidence: delete the redundant first clause. Replace 'Both coefficients were fitted to observed WMM pole strengths — Both coefficients were fitted to observed pole-region field strengths — A=62,376 nT' with 'Both coefficients were fitted to observed pole-region field strengths: A=62,376 nT'. Single-token-equivalent edit (delete one phrase + adjust em-dash to colon). Cannot regress meaning. Recommend self-apply by decider.

holes_found.description: ISS-1413 ('WIN-053 c8 H4: TLDR/P3 inconsistency on southern comparator') appears resolved in current text (TLDR and P3 both now use geographic-pole comparators, both report 13–18% overshoot) but is still listed as open in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json. The actual issue described in ISS-1413's text — internal inconsistency between detail_evidence paragraph 2 and the formula — appears to have been addressed by the same c4bfd802 symmetrize patch. Recommend decider verify and close as 'fixed by c4bfd802'.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider: cross-check ISS-1413 description against current TLDR + detail_evidence P2/P3, and if internal consistency now holds (which inspection this run suggests it does), close ISS-1413 with resolution 'fixed by c4bfd802 (Apr 23), verified WIN-053.c10 (2026-05-10)'.

holes_found.description: STILL-OPEN TRACKED ITEMS (not advanced by queue_id 327 or by the c4bfd802 batch): ISS-1243 (c5 H3, sub-terranean return-channel defender rescue not preempted), ISS-1244 (c5 H6, 3,000 km aperture sensitivity row), ISS-1245 (c5 H7, flux direction polarity), ISS-1246 (c5 H8, Sub-H(r) δ=6,371 km sourcing), ISS-1305 (potential-field continuation phrasing — assigned-analyst), ISS-1307 (EXP-194 §6.1 cross-reference missing), ISS-1446 (c9 H2 toroidal concession incomplete), ISS-1447 (c9 H3 λ-vs-A/C kind distinction). All assigned-analyst or open. None of these were addressed by the ISS-1306 patch — recording for tracker hygiene.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action this cycle. Items remain in the analyst expansion queue / open-issues backlog.

kernel_of_truth.description: ISS-1306 itself was a real and well-spotted issue — the original 'thousands of kilometres of rock' phrasing was geologically imprecise: crust is only 7–35 km thick, so a thousands-of-km flux transit traverses mantle, not crust. The patch adopting 'mantle and crust' is geologically accurate (data/wins.json:053.detail_evidence current text). Likewise, the dome model's toroidal-architecture move (V51.0 vs V50.6's monopolar) is a recognition that a single source-pole cannot reproduce a dipolar field — that's genuine physical reasoning, even if the execution fails (data/wins.json:053.detail_verdict_text opening sentence).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Geological accuracy in our rebuttal text doesn't change the underlying contradiction it's rebutting: the dome's toroidal geometry, however it routes flux, must satisfy ∇·B=0 around its own closed circuit, and the fitted B(r) violates this by ~1,600× regardless of whether the transit medium is rock, mantle, or aether (data/wins.json:053.detail_evidence flux-conservation paragraph). The 'mantle and crust' improvement makes our defender-rebuttal more credible, not the dome's claim. Similarly, recognizing the dipolar-field problem (the kernel of truth in V50.6→V51.0) doesn't validate the V51.0 solution — recognizing a problem and solving it correctly are different skills, and the fitted-coefficient B(r) reduces toroidal architecture to a curve fit with no derivation from disc geometry (verified above: monitoring='none', derives_from_dome=false).

deeper_analysis:
Verification of queue_id 327 (ISS-1306 patch) confirms the rock→mantle-and-crust replacement landed cleanly in the multi-layer-conduction defender-rebuttal paragraph. The substantive scope of this verification, however, has expanded because the WIN saw multiple uncoordinated patches between c9 (Apr 22) and now (May 10) — most notably c4bfd802's symmetrize-to-geographic-poles edit on Apr 23, which went un-reviewed. That earlier edit fixed two real findings (c9 H0 major TLDR, c9 H1 moderate P3) but introduced one new defect (the duplicated TLDR clause flagged here as the primary new hole). Net: the WIN is in better shape on substance than at c9, but worse shape on TLDR polish. The 17-day delay between the duplication landing and being flagged is a process gap — the c10 verification trigger came from queue_id 327 (ISS-1306), but the ISS-1306 patch is editorially trivial; nothing forced a TLDR re-read until this run.

Advocate-mode (other side): a dome defender's strongest move is now to point at the duplication and the residual still-open ISS list (1243–1246, 1305, 1307, 1446, 1447). The duplication is editorially embarrassing but not factually wrong; once de-duplicated, the substantive argument is stronger than at c9 (both comparators geographic, no SMP/NMP cherry-pick, geological depth language now accurate). The defender's strongest substantive move remains ISS-1243 (sub-terranean return-channel rescue). That defender path is acknowledged in detail_evidence ('the defender's strongest remaining move'), but the rebuttal is currently distributed across paragraphs rather than concentrated — ISS-1243's expansion will tighten this. None of the still-open items rise to the level of refuting our verdict; they're polishing items.

Note on c9 H4 (still-open tracked items): all four c5 ISSs (1243–1246) remain in the analyst expansion queue. This is not a process failure — they're moderate/minor items competing with higher-priority work. Recording the list here so the tracker reflects current state.

citations_verified: WMM2025 / IGRF-13: ~55,000 nT at geographic poles is the standard tabulated value (NCEI/NOAA WMM and IGRF datasets, both linked in the WIN text)
Disc/firmament parameters disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km, moon_altitude=2,534 km — match CLAUDE.md core parameters
Doodson-style citation (Griffiths Introduction to Electrodynamics §5.3.3 Example 5.12) — standard textbook reference, used here as illustrative not load-bearing
λ=8,619 km appearing in both H(r) and B(r) — confirmed against detail_evidence parameter-sharing paragraph
ISS-1306 closed-issues entry shows status 'fixed' and matches the patch landing in current text
monitor.py contains no WIN-053 domain — confirmed via grep this run
citations_unchecked: Centered-dipole calculation B(θ≈170°) ≈ 60,700 nT — arithmetic uses moment m≈7.94e22 A·m² (standard value); not re-derived this run, treated as inherited from c8 verification

summary_for_decider: ISS-1306 patch landed cleanly — verdict (Self-Contradicted) holds; confidence 0.85. Primary new finding: c4bfd802's TLDR symmetrize edit (Apr 23) introduced a duplicated em-dashed lead-in clause ('Both coefficients were fitted to observed WMM pole strengths — Both coefficients were fitted to observed pole-region field strengths —') that survived 17 days un-flagged. Severity moderate (editorial defect, not factual). Single-token-equivalent fix recommended for self-apply. Two side-effects: c9 H0 (major TLDR) and c9 H1 (moderate P3) appear resolved by c4bfd802 — recommend decider close ISS-1413 with resolution attribution. Eight ISSs remain in analyst expansion queue / open-issues (1243–1246, 1305, 1307, 1446, 1447), none addressed by this patch run. Batched (position 2 of 2) — see WIN-050.c5.json for batch position 1. No batch abort needed: all findings here are minor or moderate, no severity upgrade.
